cross_scene_arcs.title: Romantic Relationship with Stew
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Anne's relationship with Stew evolves from initial amusement to passionate romance and marriage, but conflicts over independence, jealousy, and public scandal lead to separation and divorce proceedings.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Anne is suspicious but amused by Stew.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Stew's refusal to accept financial settlement from Anne.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Conflict with Mother
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Anne's relationship with her mother deteriorates as she moves from mediating to appease her to openly defying her disapproval and defending Stew.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Anne mediates to calm her mother after Stew's visit.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Anne sides with Stew against her mother's criticism, deepening the rift.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Mother's ongoing disapproval of the marriage.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Public Image and Marital Status
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Anne's marital status changes from single to married to divorcing, accompanied by public scandal that affects her social standing.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Anne's marriage to Stew is publicly announced.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Anne is pursuing divorce amid public scandal.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Handling the fallout from the scandal and divorce.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Unrequited Feelings and Initial Strain
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gallagher reacts with jealousy to Stew's romantic interest, is devastated by his marriage announcement, and begins distancing herself emotionally.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Jealous and strained friendship with Stew
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Forlorn and struggling to maintain control while distancing from Stew
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her unrequited feelings for Stew and how to cope with his marriage

cross_scene_arcs.title: Navigating Friendship Amidst Marriage
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gallagher faces Anne's jealousy during a society event, reconciles with Stew through apology, and experiences a shift in interaction after Stew reveals Anne's ignorance of her gender.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: In awkward exchange with Stew and his hostile wife Anne
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Stew treats Gallagher with gender-aware behavior after learning she is a woman
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Anne's ongoing jealousy and its impact on Gallagher's friendship with Stew

cross_scene_arcs.title: Supportive Role and Creative Bond
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gallagher agrees to help Stew when he is stranded and later inspires him to write a play about his life, deepening their bond through support and creativity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Joyfully preparing to help Stew by contacting Hank
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Becomes a source of creative inspiration for Stew's play
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Stew will proceed with writing the play based on her suggestion

cross_scene_arcs.title: Romantic Realization
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Stew chooses to leave with Gallagher after an argument with Anne, and they share a passionate kiss, indicating mutual romantic feelings.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Leaving the Schuyler house with Stew as his designated exit partner
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Emotional, crying, and held by Stew after a prolonged kiss
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The future of their romantic relationship after the kiss

cross_scene_arcs.title: Pursuit and Marriage to Anne
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Stew pursues Anne romantically, builds trust by returning letters, deepens their bond, marries her, but faces marital conflicts over social differences and friendships, culminating in him leaving after an ultimatum.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Stew decides to call on Anne romantically.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Stew leaves the Schuyler home after demanding Anne take his surname.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Anne will accept Stew's condition for reconciliation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Evolving Bond with Gallagher
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gallagher's initial jealousy over Stew's pursuit of Anne evolves as Stew prioritizes their friendship, Gallagher inspires Stew's writing, and they share an emotional kiss during a creative moment.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Gallagher is jealous of Stew's romantic interest in Anne.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Stew and Gallagher share a passionate kiss while reenacting a play scene.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The impact of the kiss on their friendship and future interactions.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Professional Setback to Creative Inspiration
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Stew's professional reputation is damaged by his marriage scoop and public ridicule, leading to writer's block, but he finds inspiration to write a play about his life with Anne after Gallagher's suggestion.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Stew is reprimanded for letting his marriage be scooped and faces professional shame.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Stew is excitedly brainstorming a new play based on his personal life.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Stew can complete the play and regain professional standing.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Rejection by Mrs. Schuyler and Marital Ultimatum
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Mrs. Schuyler orders Stew to leave her home, and later, after marital tensions, Stew confronts her and Anne, asserting his identity by demanding Anne take his surname before departing.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Stew is forced to leave the Schuyler home by Mrs. Schuyler.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Stew leaves the Schuyler house again, this time with Gallagher, after giving Anne an ultimatum.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Anne will comply with Stew's demand to be called 'Mrs. Stew Smith.'
